Content: Offscreen Voice: Tomorrow, how's it going to play out?
Content_zh: 畫外音：明天會怎麼走？

Content: Schafer: We're going to, let's see, tomorrow, come in here, push the button, there's a big plastic button in the middle of the room.
Content_zh: Schafer：我們明天會，讓我想想，來這裡按按鈕，房間中央有個大型塑膠按鈕。

Content: We all push it at the same time, put our hands in and then push it.
Content_zh: 大家同時按，把手伸過去，然後一起按。

Content: We totally should have that.
Content_zh: 我們真的該弄一個。

Content: That'd be much more dramatic than like Greg sitting at his desk and Chris and then they tap and then they're like, OK.
Content_zh: 那比 Greg 坐在桌前，Chris 也在，然後他們點一下說「好了」有戲劇性多了。

Content: It's off.
Content_zh: 上線了。

Content: But that's the way it's going to go.
Content_zh: 但實際上就是會那樣。

Content: [Music] Gardner: There's so much animation that needs to be polished.
Content_zh: [音樂] Gardner：有太多動畫需要打磨。

Content: Crook: Well, well, there's a lot that's, that's done, but it's just not in.
Content_zh: Crook：嗯，有很多其實已經做完了，只是還沒放進去。

Content: Somewhat slow going.
Content_zh: 進度有點慢。

Content: Schafer: OK. Does anyone want to say anything about the schedule?
Content_zh: Schafer：好，有人想談談時程嗎？

Content: Like how easy it looks?
Content_zh: 比方說看起來有多簡單？

Content: Rice: But yeah, there's a lot to do in two months left.
Content_zh: Rice：只剩兩個月了，還有很多事要做。

Content: Schafer: Where's the warmest spot in this, in this office?
Content_zh: Schafer：這裡最暖和的地方在哪？

Content: Crook: Just snuggle up to me.
Content_zh: Crook：靠過來抱我就好。

Content: Crook: This sweater and, Schafer: Oh, wow.
Content_zh: Crook：這件毛衣還有，Schafer：喔，哇。

Content: He's so warm in there.
Content_zh: 他在裡面好暖和。

Content: Look at you, you look like a longshoreman.
Content_zh: 看看你，你看起來像碼頭工人。

Content: Crook: I was so cold yesterday.
Content_zh: Crook：我昨天冷死了。

Content: Schafer: You're like a lumberjack, like a lumberjack by the sea.
Content_zh: Schafer：你像個伐木工，像海邊的伐木工。

Content: I just need to be by Anna's heater.
Content_zh: 我只需要待在 Anna 的暖爐旁邊。

Content: Schafer: Hey, is this thing even on?
Content_zh: Schafer：嘿，這東西有開嗎？

Content: Kipnis: You want it on? Here.
Content_zh: Kipnis：你想開嗎？來。

Content: Schafer: Gather 'round the fire, everybody.
Content_zh: Schafer：大家圍到火邊來。

Content: Rice: We each should have a little fire.
Content_zh: Rice：我們每個人都該有一小團火。

Content: Schafer: Hello. Is everyone here?
Content_zh: Schafer：嗨，大家都到了嗎？

Content: We'll have him, quick. OK, anyways.
Content_zh: 我們很快讓他過來。好，總之。

Content: Hey, everybody. What are you doing?
Content_zh: 嘿，各位。你們在做什麼？

Content: Mr. Petty, what have you been working on?
Content_zh: Petty 先生，你最近在忙什麼？

Content: Petty: What we're doing up until, for the next couple weeks is getting all the new content
Content_zh: Petty：接下來幾週我們要做的事，就是把所有新內容都放進去。

Content: and, which I think in art, there's very little new content.
Content_zh: 不過以美術來說，我覺得新內容其實很少。

Content: It's mainly us repainting and polishing and refining.
Content_zh: 主要是在重畫、打磨和調整。

Content: Ryken: Yeah, we're coming down to the wire but it's going good.
Content_zh: Ryken：對，已經到最後關頭了，但進展還不錯。

Content: I'm working on little fix-y things.
Content_zh: 我在處理一些小修小補。

Content: Things that were painted really crappily and are now, I'm in the time to polish up.
Content_zh: 那些畫得很糟的東西，現在終於輪到我來打磨了。

Content: Schafer: Bagel's right here.
Content_zh: Schafer：Bagel 就在這裡。

Content: Ryken: Yes, yes.
Content_zh: Ryken：對，對。

Content: I'm fixing, Petty: You better be referring to other work.
Content_zh: Ryken：我在修，Petty：你最好是在說別人的東西。

Content: Ryken: I'm fixing the things that Bagel's "fixing", right now.
Content_zh: Ryken：我現在正在修 Bagel「修好」的東西。

Content: Ryken: We're just getting down to the point where, [Offscreen Voice]
Content_zh: Ryken：我們現在已經進到那個階段，就是……[畫外音]

Content: Ryken: What?
Content_zh: Ryken：什麼？

Content: Ryken: Oh, look at that! That's crazy.
Content_zh: Ryken：喔，看看那個！太扯了。

Content: Didn't I just do that. Petty: Yeah.
Content_zh: 我剛剛不是才做過嗎？Petty：對啊。

Content: Ryken: He's so fast, man.
Content_zh: Ryken：老兄，他動作也太快了。

Content: You have the rim light working on him?
Content_zh: 你把他身上的輪廓光弄好了嗎？

Content: Man, maybe I'm just slow.
Content_zh: 老兄，也許只是我太慢了。

Content: Petty: In some cases we're repeating characters, because now that we see like the range of what they did,
Content_zh: Petty：有些角色我們會重做，因為現在看得出來它們需要做到什麼程度。

Content: like we repainted a Mog Chothra, Levi did for like the third time.
Content_zh: 像 Mog Chothra，我們就重畫了，大概是 Levi 第三次畫了。

Content: And part of that is because his presence in the game was much bigger than when we originally painted him.
Content_zh: 其中一部分原因是，他在遊戲裡的存在感比我們一開始畫他時大得多。

Content: And the type of stuff he needs to do for like the boss battle or for these other areas
Content_zh: 而且他在 boss 戰或其他區域需要做的動作，

Content: is a greater range of movement and a lot more close-ups and has various mouth states.
Content_zh: 需要更大的動作範圍、更多特寫，還有各種嘴型狀態。

Content: So the best thing to do is like sometimes you can keep patching asset, but sometimes it's best to just go, wipe that clean.
Content_zh: 所以最好的做法是，有時候可以繼續補素材，但有時候乾脆重來比較好。

Content: And we know exactly how it's going to be used now, just repaint it.
Content_zh: 現在我們很清楚它會怎麼用，就直接重畫。

Content: So that's pretty much what we did.
Content_zh: 所以我們差不多就是這麼做的。

Content: And it'll pay off, because he has a lot of screen time.
Content_zh: 這會值得的，因為他的出場時間很多。

Content: Ryken: Yeah, we're not as pressed as the animators.
Content_zh: Ryken：對，我們不像動畫師那麼趕。

Content: We kind of just listen to them, working their ass off over there while we kind of just pretty things up.
Content_zh: 我們就聽著他們在那邊拚命趕工，而我們只是把東西弄漂亮一點。

Content: Gardner: Today I'm trying to get this scene in, 'cause it's such a big one.
Content_zh: Gardner：今天我想把這個場景做進去，因為這場戲規模很大。

Content: It's going to be a series of like four different cut scenes that we put together in the game.
Content_zh: 這會是我們放進遊戲裡、由四段不同過場動畫組成的一整段。

Content: And like none of those are in the game right now, and that's like, really close to shipping.
Content_zh: 但那些現在一段都還沒進遊戲，而我們已經非常接近發行了。

Content: So it's like this last, bit of time.
Content_zh: 所以就是剩下最後這一點時間。

Content: Just like every other department, we're all kinda just like rushing through everything.
Content_zh: 就像其他部門一樣，我們大家都有點在趕著把所有東西做完。

Content: Choy: The animation, like overall, like animation just has more work, but a lot of times my time is compressed into like the last,
Content_zh: Choy：整體來說，動畫的工作量就是比較多，但很多時候，我能工作的時間都被壓到最後，

Content: like couple of weeks that I can do work, because I come after everyone else and like I guess, right before audio.
Content_zh: 最後幾週才輪到我做，因為我排在大家後面，大概就在音效之前。

Content: Stoddard: I'm finishing all the sound in two weeks. Schafer: Yay!
Content_zh: Stoddard：我要在兩週內完成所有音效。Schafer：耶！

Content: Stoddard: I'm going, I'm basically following the animators as closely as possible, because I'm trying to jump on all the cut scenes as they come in and like do them.
Content_zh: Stoddard：我基本上盡量緊跟動畫師，因為每段過場動畫一進來，我就要馬上接手處理。

Content: Schafer: Ray's like, good luck, 'cause we're so fast.
Content_zh: Schafer：Ray 大概會說祝你好運，因為我們動作太快了。

Content: Stoddard: I'm trying to save January 6 through 10 to mix the game.
Content_zh: Stoddard：我想把 1 月 6 到 10 號留給遊戲混音。

Content: I'd really, really like to have that time to do that, so I'm trying to get in as much as possible in the next two weeks.
Content_zh: 我真的很想保留那段時間，所以接下來兩週我會盡量多做一點。

Content: Choy: Kind even Camden and I had a lunch where we were kind of talking about like, OK, well, we're here at this stage now
Content_zh: Choy：Camden 和我有次吃午餐時就在聊：「好，我們現在已經到這個階段了。」

Content: where everything's supposed to be wrapping up.
Content_zh: 照理說所有東西都該收尾了。

Content: Like what do we do?
Content_zh: 我們該怎麼辦？

Content: Like what's the social fix for it?
Content_zh: 有沒有什麼人際上的解法？

Content: And like part of me's like, oh, you know, like I'm just going to be really like irritable all the time
Content_zh: 我有一部分會想，喔，那我就一直表現得很易怒好了。

Content: and just like be really hard, kind of hard to like work with.
Content_zh: 讓自己變得很難搞、很難共事。

Content: So like if people want to make changes they'll be like, oh my God, did I piss her off even more?
Content_zh: 這樣如果有人想改東西，就會想：天啊，我是不是又惹她更生氣了？

Content: So like this, like some of that, like there's like you kind of have to be a little bit of a wall,
Content_zh: 所以有時候你得稍微當一堵牆，

Content: otherwise like, just so people, not necessarily to like stop changes, but just so people will like kind of think twice
Content_zh: 不一定是為了阻止改動，而是讓大家想改東西前會多想一下，

Content: or like maybe think smarter when they want to polish something.
Content_zh: 或是在想打磨某個東西時更聰明一點。

Content: Schafer: So it's a little tight.
Content_zh: Schafer：所以時間有點緊。

Content: This means more late night food for free for Ray.
Content_zh: 這代表 Ray 可以多吃幾頓免費宵夜。

Content: See, so it's really saving Ray money, 'cause he's going to stay late to animate all this stuff.
Content_zh: 你看，這其實是在幫 Ray 省錢，因為他會留下來加班，把這些東西都做動畫。

Content: Rice: And this is just a bunch of rice.
Content_zh: Rice：這只是一堆 rice。

Content: Crook: You see, this is the size.
Content_zh: Crook：你看，這是大小。

Content: Schafer: I've been doing a lot of press stuff.
Content_zh: Schafer：我最近做了很多媒體宣傳。

Content: We went down and showed the game at the VGX Awards.
Content_zh: 我們去 VGX Awards 展示了遊戲。

Content: Did you guys watch?
Content_zh: 你們看了嗎？

Content: It was fun.
Content_zh: 很好玩。

Content: I got a lot of love on Twitter from that.
Content_zh: 那次之後，我在 Twitter 上收到很多好評。

Content: And a lot of people who hadn't heard of the game before were just crazy, because I always assume the entire world knows all about the game.
Content_zh: 很多以前沒聽過這款遊戲的人都很興奮，因為我總是假設全世界都知道這款遊戲。

Content: But I got this like, what is this game?
Content_zh: 但我看到很多「這是什麼遊戲？」之類的反應。

Content: This looks kind of cool, this is awesome.
Content_zh: 「看起來滿酷的，太棒了。」

Content: Keighley: This is really exciting, 'cause this is a guy I've loved for so long and we now have him live on the VGX stage.
Content_zh: Keighley：這真的很令人興奮，因為這位是我喜歡很久的人，現在他來到 VGX 舞台現場。

Content: Not you, Joel, but Tim Schafer is with us!
Content_zh: 不是你啦，Joel，是 Tim Schafer 來到現場！

Content: Tim Schafer!
Content_zh: Tim Schafer！

Content: Tim is of course the creative genius behind Brutal Legend, Psychonauts and Grim Fandango
Content_zh: Tim 當然是《Brütal Legend》、《Psychonauts》和《Grim Fandango》背後的創意天才。

Content: and his latest is the Kickstarter funded adventure game, Broken Age, which raised over three million dollars.
Content_zh: 他的最新作品是由 Kickstarter 資助的冒險遊戲《Broken Age》，募得超過 300 萬美元。

Content: McHale: Three and a half million, Geoff.
Content_zh: McHale：三百五十萬啦，Geoff。

Content: Schafer: Thank you.
Content_zh: Schafer：謝謝。

Content: Schafer: He's funny. He's nice.
Content_zh: Schafer：他很有趣，人也很好。

Content: He was nice to me off camera.
Content_zh: 鏡頭外他對我很好。

Content: He was like, hey, get ready for this.
Content_zh: 他就說：「嘿，先有個心理準備。」

Content: I'm going to ask you if you were high when you designed this game.
Content_zh: 「我要問你設計這款遊戲時是不是嗑藥了。」

Content: McHale: @Brandon Victoran asks, hey, Tim.
Content_zh: McHale：@Brandon Victoran 問，嘿，Tim。

Content: What was the inspiration for the project?
Content_zh: 這個專案的靈感是什麼？

Content: Schafer: Well, McHale: Did you get high or something?
Content_zh: Schafer：嗯，McHale：你是嗑藥了還是怎樣？

Content: Schafer: Uh, no, no. McHale: Sorry.
Content_zh: Schafer：呃，沒有，沒有。McHale：抱歉。

Content: Remo: It's been getting really tangible, how excited people are to play it.
Content_zh: Remo：大家有多期待玩到它，現在變得很具體、很明顯。

Content: Like it's been very visible and you can really, you can really feel it.
Content_zh: 這種期待很明顯，你真的感受得到。

Content: So it's very, very exciting, like we have a big list of stuff to do to get the game out.
Content_zh: 所以真的非常令人興奮，不過要把遊戲推出去，我們還有一大串事情要做。

Content: You know, Greg is obviously driving that stuff so it's going to be pretty crazy few weeks,
Content_zh: Greg 顯然在主導這些事，所以接下來幾週會很瘋狂。

Content: but I think our community is going to be incredibly enthusiastic.
Content_zh: 但我覺得我們的社群會非常熱烈支持。

Content: Is going to, and really help us get the word out.
Content_zh: 而且真的會幫我們把消息傳出去。

Content: So it's really good times.
Content_zh: 所以現在狀況很不錯。

Content: Rice: I think that there's going to be a lot of excitement.
Content_zh: Rice：我覺得大家會很興奮。

Content: Like I think we've been doing some bigger things, like VGX and stuff that's been building up, you know, hype.
Content_zh: 我覺得我們最近做了一些比較大的宣傳，像 VGX 之類的，慢慢把聲勢炒起來。

Content: And I think that ultimately the people who are going to care the most about the game are the 90,000 that already bought it
Content_zh: 我覺得到頭來最在乎這款遊戲的人，就是已經買下它的那九萬人。

Content: and they're going to be super-stoked playing it.
Content_zh: 他們玩到時一定會超興奮。

Content: So I feel like there's going to be a huge ripple effect from them, of them just sharing their excitement.
Content_zh: 所以我覺得他們只要分享自己的興奮，就會帶出很大的漣漪效應。

Content: I think that that'll just be something that we should try and capitalize on as much as possible.
Content_zh: 我想這是我們應該盡量把握的機會。

Content: Bailey: the big splash, so you're thinking?
Content_zh: Bailey：所以你想的是製造大聲勢？

Content: Rice: Yeah, well, I just think that yeah, they've been waiting 15 years for this game.
Content_zh: Rice：對，我只是覺得他們已經等這款遊戲等了 15 年。

Content: Bailey: There is the counterpoint, just so you know, which is that, that all the adventure fans are really interested in this
Content_zh: Bailey：只是也有另一種看法。你要知道，冒險遊戲粉絲確實都很關注這款遊戲，

Content: and it's a very small market and that's why most publishers got out of it.
Content_zh: 但這是個很小的市場，所以大多數發行商都退出了。

Content: Rice: Yeah, I know.
Content_zh: Rice：對，我知道。

Content: Bailey: So those fans have basically already backed the game.
Content_zh: Bailey：所以那些粉絲基本上都已經贊助這款遊戲了。

Content: Rice: Yeah. But that's what we're going to prove wrong, right?
Content_zh: Rice：對，但我們就是要證明那是錯的，對吧？

Content: Bailey: You're going to prove that wrong, OK.
Content_zh: Bailey：你會證明我錯了，好。

Content: Rice: Everyone is a skeptic.
Content_zh: Rice：大家都很懷疑。

Content: Bailey: But it still could, it can be somewhere between it, right?
Content_zh: Bailey：但結果也可能落在中間，對吧？

Content: Where is the genre, is say, you know, 500,000 people.
Content_zh: 這個類型的市場也許介於中間，比方說五十萬人。

Content: Rice: Yeah, I think that that's where the quality of the game is going to be, ultimately makes a call.
Content_zh: Rice：對，我覺得到最後還是遊戲品質會決定結果。

Content: Like I think if it comes out and reviews really well and people have a really great experience and are talking about it,
Content_zh: 我覺得如果遊戲推出後評價很好，玩家體驗也很棒，大家都在討論它，

Content: I think the game will be able to kind of live on through really solid sales.
Content_zh: 我想這遊戲就能靠很穩定的銷售長久活下去。

Content: You know, if it has kind of average scores, then it probably will be a different story.
Content_zh: 如果評分普普通通，那可能就是另一回事了。

Content: Schafer: Broken Age is a welcome return to the graphic adventure games by Tim Schafer.
Content_zh: Schafer：《Broken Age》是 Tim Schafer 回歸圖像冒險遊戲的可喜之作。

Content: See, I can't read this, it's got my name in it.
Content_zh: 你看，我念不下去，裡面有我的名字。

Content: Schafer: We hired some old friends of ours who are game reviewers in the previous life
Content_zh: Schafer：我們請了一些老朋友，他們以前當過遊戲評測。

Content: and asked them to write a review of Broken Age as if they were actually, just got a copy and were reviewing it.
Content_zh: 請他們像真的拿到評測版一樣，寫一篇《Broken Age》的評論。

Content: To know, you know, is there some big thing we have overlooked or what, what might a review look like for the game.
Content_zh: 想知道我們是不是漏掉了什麼大問題，還有這款遊戲的評論可能會長什麼樣子。

Content: And they came back really good.
Content_zh: 結果回來的評價很不錯。

Content: Positives: intuitive point and click interface, good balance between exploration, character interaction and puzzle solving.
Content_zh: 優點：直覺的點擊式介面，探索、角色互動與解謎之間取得良好平衡。

Content: Game recognizes when players are stuck and provides clues without giving them answers.
Content_zh: 遊戲能辨識玩家卡關，並提供線索而不是直接給答案。

Content: Negatives: I missed the inventory command and the opening with Shay and the spoon.
Content_zh: 缺點：我懷念物品欄指令，也不喜歡 Shay 和湯匙的開場。

Content: Phfft. In the gaming industry mock reviews are a common thing.
Content_zh: 噗。在遊戲業，模擬評測是很常見的事。

Content: We have never done our own, so that's the good thing.
Content_zh: 我們以前沒自己做過，所以這也算好事。

Content: It's like a part of what we're trying to do is being a real self-publisher.
Content_zh: 這有點像是我們想成為真正自我發行商的一部分。

Content: You know, being your own publisher is taking care of these things, which are like mock reviews,
Content_zh: 自己當發行商，就得處理這類事情，像是模擬評測。

Content: 'cause they affect how much or how you plan your launch, you know.
Content_zh: 因為它們會影響你要多大規模、或怎麼規劃上市。

Content: Like you would plan, if we got really like, if the mock reviews were like, ah, it's kind of rough,
Content_zh: 比如說，如果模擬評測回來的結果是：嗯，有點粗糙，

Content: but they'll probably have it sorted out by the time they launch Act Two.
Content_zh: 但他們大概會在第二幕推出前整理好，

Content: We might think that wouldn't, we'd be inclined to put it in the early, early access section of Steam, you know.
Content_zh: 我們可能就會傾向把它放到 Steam 的 Early Access 區。

Content: But if it came back, oh, it's super-polished and it's great.
Content_zh: 但如果回來的意見是：喔，它打磨得很好，很棒。

Content: It's like a first episode of something, and that means it really should be in the other section of the Steam, with regular games.
Content_zh: 它只是像某個系列的第一集，那就代表它應該放在 Steam 另一區，跟一般遊戲放一起。

Content: Schafer: No.
Content_zh: Schafer：不。

Content: I mean, Microsoft did play tests for Psychonauts and people were like, it got like fun-ativity ratings or whatever.
Content_zh: 我是說，Microsoft 當年有替《Psychonauts》做試玩測試，還有什麼「趣味度」評分之類的。

Content: And oh God.
Content_zh: 天啊。

Content: Is that what this is?
Content_zh: 是這樣嗎？

Content: Schafer: Here's the marketing document that Microsoft made for Psychonauts.
Content_zh: Schafer：這是 Microsoft 為《Psychonauts》做的行銷文件。

Content: Based on the twelve focus groups conducted worldwide, the primary weakness of the game are the story line,
Content_zh: 根據全球 12 場焦點團體，遊戲的主要弱點是故事線。

Content: which is considered unoriginal, childish and overly complex.
Content_zh: 故事被認為缺乏原創性、幼稚，而且過度複雜。

Content: Tongue-in-cheek nature of the game is a weakness, most simply did not get the joke.
Content_zh: 遊戲那種半開玩笑的調性是弱點，多數人單純沒看懂笑點。

Content: Others disliked the satirical humor.
Content_zh: 其他人不喜歡這種諷刺幽默。

Content: In addition, many feel the humor would get old limiting replayability.
Content_zh: 另外，許多人覺得幽默會很快膩，限制重玩性。

Content: Humor should be incorporated into the game as a secondary or supporting characteristic.
Content_zh: 幽默應該作為次要或輔助特色融入遊戲。

Content: Humor should be simplified and made immediately apparent.
Content_zh: 幽默應該簡化，並且一看就懂。

Content: [Laughter] To help international appeal, limit game components
Content_zh: [笑聲] 為了增加國際吸引力，應限制那些

Content: that feel especially Americanized, i.e. summer camp.
Content_zh: 限制特別美式的遊戲元素，例如夏令營。

Content: Thank you, marketing research.
Content_zh: 謝謝你，市場研究。

Content: Yeah. Limit, so you can obviously take this stuff too far.
Content_zh: 對，限制。很明顯，這種東西也可能做過頭。

Content: I mean, good God.
Content_zh: 天啊。

Content: Thank God we got cancelled. Anyway.
Content_zh: 幸好我們被取消了。總之。

Content: That's not what we're doing this time.
Content_zh: 這次我們不是在做那種事。

Content: This time we just sent it to three reviewers that we knew and asked them to write reviews, just as if they're writing actual reviews.
Content_zh: 這一次我們只是把它寄給三位認識的評測，請他們像真的在寫評測一樣寫。

Content: As mean as you would have been.
Content_zh: 就像你們真的會寫的那麼毒。

Content: If you've ever loved the Tim Schafer game, if you've ever loved whimsy, you will love this game.
Content_zh: 如果你曾經喜歡 Tim Schafer 的遊戲，如果你曾經喜歡奇想風格，你會愛上這款遊戲。

Content: I'm glad to see the return of the genre and the return of a legend.
Content_zh: 我很高興看到這個類型回歸，也看到一位傳奇回歸。

Content: See why I can't read these?
Content_zh: 知道我為什麼不能看這些嗎？

Content: That's just embarrassing.
Content_zh: 那真的很尷尬。

Content: But it's good.
Content_zh: 不過是好事。

Content: Yeah, there's no like, like it was always problematic near the end of Brutal to hear the recurring stuff about the RTS stuff,
Content_zh: 對，這次不像《Brütal Legend》後期那樣，一直聽到大家反覆提 RTS 的問題，這很麻煩。

Content: 'cause that's really hard, like what are we going to do?
Content_zh: 因為那真的很難處理，我們能怎麼辦？

Content: What the crap, what are we going to do?
Content_zh: 搞什麼鬼，我們該怎麼辦？

Content: And EA was like, we're just going to, it's good.
Content_zh: EA 就說，我們就這樣吧，沒問題。

Content: We're going to ship it how it is.
Content_zh: 我們就照這樣推出吧。

Content: But here there's nothing about the quality of game.
Content_zh: 但這次沒有針對遊戲品質的問題。

Content: It's more about, we have to hit the pricing right and we have to make sure the puzzles are hard enough, especially in the second act.
Content_zh: 比較像是我們得把定價抓準，也要確保謎題夠難，尤其是第二幕。

Content: As pricing will directly affect the overall Metacritic score breakdown of price to Metacritic score has been included, see page 8.
Content_zh: 由於定價會直接影響整體 Metacritic 分數，已附上價格對 Metacritic 分數的分析，請見第 8 頁。

Content: For the purpose of this summary will assume a $15 to $20 price point.
Content_zh: 本摘要將假設價格落在 15 到 20 美元。

Content: Oh, I don't think that's what Justin's assuming.
Content_zh: 喔，我覺得 Justin 應該不是這樣估的。

Content: He thinks if we charge $15, we'll get 80-85 Metacritic.
Content_zh: 他覺得如果賣 15 美元，我們的 Metacritic 會拿 80 到 85 分。

Content: If we charge $20, we'll get 79 to 80 percent Metacritic.
Content_zh: 如果賣 20 美元，Metacritic 會是 79 到 80 分。

Content: Charge $25 to $30, we'll go to 70 to 60 percent of Metacritic.
Content_zh: 賣 25 到 30 美元，Metacritic 就會掉到 60 到 70 分。

Content: Interesting.
Content_zh: 有意思。

Content: Schafer: That's just him saying, I mean reviewers, I guess, take into account how much a game costs when they give it a score.
Content_zh: Schafer：他只是說，評測在打分數時，我猜會把遊戲價格也納入考量。

Content: So it costs $60, you know, like geometry wars.
Content_zh: 如果它賣 60 美元，比如《Geometry Wars》。

Content: If that cost $60, you might give it a lower score than if it cost $15.
Content_zh: 如果它賣 60 美元，你可能會比它賣 15 美元時給更低的分數。

Content: Schafer: We don't know yet.
Content_zh: Schafer：我們還不知道。

Content: That's the whole discussion.
Content_zh: 這就是整個討論的重點。

Content: Bailey: I know it's happening.
Content_zh: Bailey：我知道這確實會發生。

Content: I just kind of disagree that that reviewer should take in, price into consideration when making this review.
Content_zh: 我只是有點不同意評論者寫評測時應該把價格納入考量。

Content: Rice: Yeah, well, especially now since price fluctuates so much.
Content_zh: Rice：對，尤其現在價格波動那麼大。

Content: In the sense things go on sale so often and prices change and go down.
Content_zh: 遊戲常常特價，價格也會變、會降。

Content: Yeah, that's a tricky one.
Content_zh: 對，這題很棘手。

Content: Bailey: And so I saw, when I, I did my research so like it showed that indie games can be anywhere from like $2.99 to about $29.99,
Content_zh: Bailey：我做研究時看到，獨立遊戲的合理價格大概可以從 2.99 美元到 29.99 美元，

Content: as like the acceptable pricing.
Content_zh: 算是可接受的定價範圍。

Content: There was one for $34.99...
Content_zh: 有一款賣 34.99 美元……

Content: Rice: What was that?
Content_zh: Rice：是哪一款？

Content: Bailey: Well, it was the Monkey Island one that came out, that Telltale did.
Content_zh: Bailey：就是 Telltale 出的那款 Monkey Island。

Content: Rice: Of the Telltale, Tales of Monkey Island, yeah.
Content_zh: Rice：Telltale 的《Tales of Monkey Island》，對。

Content: Bailey: But I'm going to treat that as an outlier.
Content_zh: Bailey：但我會把那個當成離群值。

Content: It'll be like, OK.
Content_zh: 就當作「好吧」。

Content: The idea would be on release you would pay $24.99 and then you get both acts, Act One and Act Two.
Content_zh: 想法是上市時付 24.99 美元，然後拿到兩幕，第一幕和第二幕。

Content: Bailey: It is a small genre and those games used to sell, like the Grim Fandangos and you know, Full Throttles and stuff, used to sell like 500,000 copies.
Content_zh: Bailey：這是個小眾類型，以前那些遊戲，像《Grim Fandango》、《Full Throttle》之類，大概能賣 50 萬套。

Content: You know, there were the hundreds of thousands where there were.
Content_zh: 以前確實有賣到幾十萬套的。

Content: And you know, that's one of the reasons why if you can't sell, you know, millions upon millions now, you know, games are considered failures.
Content_zh: 而這也是原因之一：現在如果賣不到幾百萬套，遊戲就會被視為失敗。

Content: The good thing about us, is since we're publishing ourselves, you know, and it's digital distribution
Content_zh: 我們的好處是，因為我們是自己發行，又是數位發行，

Content: and we can handle almost all aspects of the publishing.
Content_zh: 而且幾乎所有發行面向我們都能自己處理。

Content: And so for us it actually doesn't have to make a huge amount of money.
Content_zh: 所以對我們來說，它其實不需要賺一大筆錢。

Content: And 500,000 copies, if it sells 500,000 copies, we'll be enormously successful as a studio.
Content_zh: 如果賣出 50 萬套，對我們這間工作室來說就是非常巨大的成功。

Content: That will supply like all the funds we need to be completely independent.
Content_zh: 那就能提供我們完全獨立所需的資金。

Content: Rice: Anyway, this is exciting.
Content_zh: Rice：總之，這很令人興奮。

Content: So, cool, man.
Content_zh: 所以，酷啊。

Content: Rice: Yeah, it's coming.
Content_zh: Rice：對，要來了。

Content: It's actually nice, 'cause when these decisions are made and you're just like committed to them,
Content_zh: 這其實不錯，因為一旦決定做下去，你也真的投入了，

Content: then you just kind of sit back and I don't know, watch things happen.
Content_zh: 就可以往後坐，然後看事情發展。

Content: Bailey: Yeah, can you imagine?
Content_zh: Bailey：你能想像嗎？

Content: What would it be like if we sold like, if we sold like blockbuster numbers for a publisher?
Content_zh: 如果我們賣出像發行商那種大片等級的數字，會是什麼感覺？

Content: God, it'd be crazy.
Content_zh: 天啊，那會很瘋狂。

Content: Yeah. Don't know.
Content_zh: 是啊。不知道。

Content: Bailey: No, I, it's, you know, basically it's the studio's dream, which is to continue to operate independently.
Content_zh: Bailey：不，我是說，這基本上就是工作室的夢想，也就是能繼續獨立運作。

Content: Bailey: But like this is, the awesome time, the awesome thing about this timeframe is that there's not a lot being featured on Steam.
Content_zh: Bailey：但這個時段很棒，最棒的是 Steam 上沒有太多主打作品。

Content: So we could potentially, best case scenario, like Max Paine, I think got, they got a month of a feature, basically.
Content_zh: 所以最理想的情況下，我想《Max Payne》基本上拿到了一整個月的主打。

Content: If we could get that from the 28th all the way through February, that would be amazing.
Content_zh: 如果我們能從 28 號一路拿到 2 月，那就太棒了。

Content: Bailey: Are you OK with that, rather than, I mean, 'cause I would love five dollars more and everything,
Content_zh: Bailey：你可以接受那樣嗎？我是說，我當然也想多賺五美元之類的，

Content: but I didn't want like, I just don't want the game to be, Schafer: No.
Content_zh: 但我不想，我只是不要讓遊戲……Schafer：不會。

Content: I mean, I feel like you guys have talked it through enough and I was nervous about it coming out at $30 and the mock reviews wanted it to be $20,
Content_zh: 我是說，我覺得你們已經討論得夠多了。我原本擔心它以 30 美元推出，而模擬評測希望它是 20 美元，

Content: so I think, Offscreen Voice: Split the difference.
Content_zh: 所以我覺得，畫外音：折衷一下。

Content: Schafer: Is this a bad time to propose not splitting the game?
Content_zh: Schafer：現在提議不要把遊戲拆成兩半，時機是不是不太好？

Content: Considered that lately? Wouldn't have to think about any of this stuff for a while.
Content_zh: 最近有考慮過嗎？這樣暫時就不用想這些事了。

Content: No, I'm excited about shipping.
Content_zh: 沒有啦，我很期待推出。

Content: I'm excited about it.
Content_zh: 我很興奮。

Content: Bailey: I think this is actually going to probably be, I think it will result in a better showing than if you had kept it all together.
Content_zh: Bailey：我覺得這樣最後的表現，可能會比你把整款遊戲合在一起更好。

Content: Schafer: I think it definitely got more people talking about the game.
Content_zh: Schafer：我覺得這確實讓更多人開始討論這款遊戲。

Content: Bailey: Yes.
Content_zh: Bailey：對。

Content: But it's like, the more opportunities you actually have to sell, actually potentially better.
Content_zh: 而且實際上，能多幾次銷售機會，可能反而比較好。

Content: Schafer: Sell each puzzle.
Content_zh: Schafer：每個謎題都拆開賣。

Content: I don't like this puzzle; I'd like to return this puzzle.
Content_zh: 我不喜歡這個謎題，我想退掉這個謎題。

Content: Bailey: Micro transactions to adventure games. Schafer: OK.
Content_zh: Bailey：把微交易帶進冒險遊戲。Schafer：好。

Content: Bailey: You know you have the fun start, about the build lockdown and timelines for all the stuff that needs to get done.
Content_zh: Bailey：接下來就是有趣的部分了：版本封版，以及所有待辦事項的時程。

Content: So when is the build, like when are we done touching it?
Content_zh: 所以這個版本什麼時候算定案？什麼時候不能再動？

Content: Rice: Yeah. I don't know.
Content_zh: Rice：對。我還不知道。

Content: That's a question that I would like to involve other people in, like Oliver.
Content_zh: 我想把其他人也拉進來討論這個問題，比如 Oliver。

Content: Offscreen Voice: So you know, what is happening at this point?
Content_zh: 畫外音：所以現在到什麼階段了？

Content: Franzke: A lot, a lot is happening.
Content_zh: Franzke：很多，真的很多事在發生。

Content: Yeah, we kind of just switched into, I guess what's often called finaling mode where it's a lot of, I'm basically going away from working on tasks
Content_zh: 對，我們算是切換到通常所謂的收尾模式；我基本上不再做新任務，

Content: and more towards fixing issues in the game.
Content_zh: 而是更多在修遊戲裡的問題。

Content: Just so that we can maybe get a stable version that can be released eventually.
Content_zh: 這樣最後也許能做出一個可以發布的穩定版本。

Content: Romero: That's a crash.
Content_zh: Romero：當掉了。

Content: Yeah, you can press "space" to skip the cut scenes.
Content_zh: 對，你可以按「空白鍵」跳過過場動畫。

Content: So in this particular case, for some, you just skip the cut scene and crash.
Content_zh: 所以在這個特定情況下，不知為什麼，只要跳過過場動畫就會當掉。

Content: Yeah, we've skipped that cut scene, I want to say thousands of times, you know, collectively.
Content_zh: 對，大家加起來應該已經跳過那段過場動畫幾千次了。

Content: And now it decides to crash for some reason.
Content_zh: 結果現在它不知為什麼決定要當掉。

Content: That's really how most bugs are found.
Content_zh: 大多數 bug 其實就是這樣被找到的。

Content: You're not trying to do something, it happens.
Content_zh: 你不是刻意去做某件事，它就突然發生了。

Content: What I've learned is that the nature of the adventure game is that it's, everything is very handcrafted, right?
Content_zh: 我學到的是，冒險遊戲的本質就是每個東西都非常手工，對吧？

Content: You want to control every step of the way because it's all story driven.
Content_zh: 你會想控制每個步驟，因為一切都是由故事推動的。

Content: And because of that, you have so many little points where it just, there's a bad graphic or the audio's off
Content_zh: 也因為這樣，會有很多小地方可能出問題，比方說圖像不對，或音訊沒對上，

Content: or characters look weird doing a particular action.
Content_zh: 或是角色做某個動作時看起來怪怪的。

Content: And because they want a very specific type of sort of experience, like to look a certain way and sound a certain way,
Content_zh: 而且因為他們想要一種很特定的體驗，比如看起來要某種樣子、聽起來要某種樣子，

Content: you just end up with a lot of little bugs in between all the big functional stuff.
Content_zh: 最後就會在那些大型功能之間冒出很多小 bug。

Content: So functionally, I mean, it's not too complex, you know, point and click.
Content_zh: 所以功能面來說，它不是太複雜，就是點擊式操作。

Content: I'd say our save load system is probably the most complicated because save load's always complicated anyway.
Content_zh: 我會說我們的存讀檔系統大概最複雜，因為存讀檔本來就很複雜。

Content: And at this point it's just polish.
Content_zh: 到了這個階段就都是打磨。

Content: Little graphics things, animation, there's a lot of missing audio, stuff like that.
Content_zh: 小圖像問題、動畫、很多缺失的音訊，諸如此類。

Content: So this is the Broken Age database.
Content_zh: 這是《Broken Age》資料庫。

Content: This is the list of all bugs.
Content_zh: 這是所有 bug 的清單。

Content: This particular issue is going to be for Oliver, which means it's a system bug.
Content_zh: 這個問題會交給 Oliver，代表它是系統 bug。

Content: And that's it.
Content_zh: 就這樣。

Content: Once you have all the detail, the title, the steps, any attachments, if you need them.
Content_zh: 填好所有細節、標題、重現步驟，需要的話再附上附件。

Content: In this case we don't.
Content_zh: 這個案例不需要。

Content: Submit the bug and off it goes.
Content_zh: 送出 bug，它就進流程了。

Content: Franzke: The QA team is very, very good at like describing exactly how they got to this and they even say sometimes oh, this doesn't happen all the time.
Content_zh: Franzke：QA 團隊非常擅長精確描述他們是怎麼遇到問題的，有時甚至會說，喔，這不是每次都會發生。

Content: So there's a problem in this particular scene where the characters occasionally will kind of, you see the jitter?
Content_zh: 這一幕有個問題，角色偶爾會有點，你看到那個抖動了嗎？

Content: See, see how this kind of like jitters? So OK.
Content_zh: 看到了嗎？看到它有點抖嗎？好。

Content: So at first, the first thing I do, I will actually look at the scene and you know, try to see the problem like this.
Content_zh: 所以一開始，我會先實際看那個場景，試著像這樣找出問題。

Content: Like oh yeah, obviously that's something going on.
Content_zh: 像是「喔對，這裡顯然有狀況。」

Content: Then we have certain tools in the game that we can use to kind of just trying to figure out what's, what's happening.
Content_zh: 然後遊戲裡有一些工具，可以用來試著釐清到底發生了什麼。

Content: I can actually pause the game in the auto-debugger that we wrote for the game.
Content_zh: 我其實可以在我們為遊戲寫的自動除錯器裡暫停遊戲。

Content: And I'm going to, you know, basically step through frame by frame in this particular case.
Content_zh: 這個案例裡，我基本上會一格一格往前檢查。

Content: And then I very often will just look at, OK, so first of all, do those numbers line up?
Content_zh: 然後我通常會先看：「好，首先，這些數字有對上嗎？」

Content: Because like, you know, the camera, animation has to be at the exact same frame, then the characters.
Content_zh: 因為鏡頭動畫必須和角色剛好在同一格。

Content: And I already see like, you know, this set of frames, 732 and this is like at the frame 734.
Content_zh: 我已經看到這組影格是 732，而這邊是在第 734 格。

Content: So like weird, why is this happening.
Content_zh: 很奇怪，為什麼會這樣？

Content: And then I basically will have to go into visual studio where they'll, you know, the code is for the game, like animation control.
Content_zh: 然後我基本上就得進 Visual Studio，看遊戲裡像動畫控制這類程式碼。

Content: A lot of what I do is actually just understanding what is going on.
Content_zh: 我做的很多事，其實只是弄懂到底發生了什麼。

Content: Like that takes 95 percent of the time.
Content_zh: 這大概會花掉 95% 的時間。

Content: Once you know what's going on, very often the fix is pretty easy, but you know, since there's so much code and so much things,
Content_zh: 一旦弄清楚發生什麼，修起來通常很簡單，但你知道，程式碼和事情都太多了，

Content: so many things going on at the same time, the understanding what is actually happening is usually the way, way, way more
Content_zh: 又有太多事情同時發生，所以真正理解狀況通常才是更、更、更困難的部分。

Content: difficult part of actually implementing a fix for it.
Content_zh: 比實際把修正做出來還難。

Content: But yeah, I mean, that's like in a nutshell what's going on.
Content_zh: 不過對，簡單說就是這樣。

Content: Romero: And then on our end, I just try to get to the tasks.
Content_zh: Romero：而我們這邊，我就是盡量把任務清掉。

Content: Like we have a lot of bug regressions right now, just 'cause they're fixing quicker than we can check them to make, to verify that they're fixed.
Content_zh: 我們現在有很多 bug 回歸測試要做，因為他們修得比我們確認修好還快。

Content: So we always have just a backlog of bugs we need to get through on that end.
Content_zh: 所以我們那邊總是有一堆待處理的 bug 積著。

Content: And new bugs.
Content_zh: 還有新的 bug。

Content: Like I said, don't stop.
Content_zh: 就像我說的，問題不會停。

Content: So at this point you can't afford to ignore any bug.
Content_zh: 所以到這個階段，任何 bug 都不能忽略。

Content: So you see it, you report it.
Content_zh: 所以看到 bug，就回報。

Content: Yeah, every time you hear a little groan or something coming from that end, that's probably every new bug.
Content_zh: 對，每次你聽到那邊傳來一點呻吟聲之類的，大概就是又有新 bug。

Content: Franzke: I think animation is winning the bug count right now.
Content_zh: Franzke：我覺得目前動畫部門的 bug 數量領先。

Content: And it probably will be until the end.
Content_zh: 而且大概會一路領先到最後。

Content: So a lot of it is just, you know, animation polish and stuff like that.
Content_zh: 所以很多都是動畫打磨之類的問題。

Content: I think they probably have like twice as many bugs, any of the other like departments.
Content_zh: 我想他們的 bug 大概是其他部門的兩倍。

Content: Roberts: Yeah, and this is all animation bugs.
Content_zh: Roberts：對，這些全都是動畫 bug。

Content: So it's something like 130.
Content_zh: 大概有 130 個。

Content: Essentially right now the red ones are unfinished cut scenes.
Content_zh: 基本上現在紅色的是尚未完成的過場動畫。

Content: And so that's what we're striving to do by tomorrow.
Content_zh: 所以這就是我們明天前努力要完成的事。

Content: They're set by priority.
Content_zh: 它們是照優先順序排的。

Content: And it doesn't, it doesn't show it here, but there's Pri-1, Pri-2, right down to 4, I think.
Content_zh: 這裡沒有顯示，但我記得有 Pri-1、Pri-2，一直到 4。

Content: Four would be like, it'd be nice to have, but not essential.
Content_zh: 4 大概就是有的話很好，但不是必要。

Content: And 3 is like we should fix it, otherwise it'll look crappy.
Content_zh: 3 就是我們應該修，不然會看起來很爛。

Content: And 2 being it is going to look really bad and unfinished if we don't do this.
Content_zh: 2 就是如果不修，會看起來很糟、很未完成。

Content: And 1 being a breaker, like it actually just breaks the game.
Content_zh: 1 就是阻斷問題，真的會讓遊戲壞掉。

Content: Gardner: When you get into bug fixing phase, a lot of time animators will be like oh, I want to,
Content_zh: Gardner：進入修 bug 階段後，動畫師很多時候會想，喔，我想要，

Content: like this is the animation that we should be polishing, but instead I'm polishing this animation because this is reported as a bug.
Content_zh: 這個才是我們該打磨的動畫，但我卻在打磨另一段，因為那段被回報成 bug。

Content: It's stuff that I'm like, that should be fixed and that should be fixed.
Content_zh: 有些東西我會覺得，這個該修、那個也該修。

Content: Nobody cares.
Content_zh: 沒人在乎。

Content: Like, I'm like, like it's like high fantasy.
Content_zh: 對我來說，這就像高奇幻一樣。

Content: Like I'm really into it, but like not everybody else is.
Content_zh: 我自己很投入，但不是每個人都這樣。

Content: Roberts: What I'm really trying to do is not get freaked out about, about things right now,
Content_zh: Roberts：我現在真正努力做的，是不要被這些事情嚇到，

Content: because the holidays are coming up and I just want to relax and rebuild my energy.
Content_zh: 因為假期快到了，我只想放鬆一下，把精力補回來。

Content: And then come back strong.
Content_zh: 然後強勢回歸。

Content: We could be panicking right now and we probably should be panicking, but I'm not going to do that.
Content_zh: 我們現在可以開始恐慌，而且也許真的該恐慌，但我不打算那麼做。

Content: I'm Zen right now.
Content_zh: 我現在很禪。

Content: I'm just going to, I'm just going to turn off and then when I come back on the second,
Content_zh: 我會先把自己關機，等我 2 號回來，

Content: that's, it'll be time to freak out for me.
Content_zh: 到時候才是我該抓狂的時候。

Content: Franzke: Again, I think the problem is to some degree, it feels like the release day is far away, but it's really not.
Content_zh: Franzke：我還是覺得問題在於，某種程度上發行日感覺很遠，但其實一點也不遠。

Content: Because it's like, oh, it's next year, right?
Content_zh: 因為聽起來會像：「喔，那是明年，對吧？」

Content: That's, you know, once you say that, we're going to release the game next year, that feels like, oh,
Content_zh: 一旦你說「我們明年要發行遊戲」，感覺就會變成：

Content: that's so far away we don't really need to worry about it too much.
Content_zh: 「那還很久，我們不用太擔心。」

Content: But I think in terms of working weeks that we have until the game ships is like two or two and a half.
Content_zh: 但如果算到遊戲推出前的工作週，大概只剩兩週或兩週半。

Content: So it's really not that much time, given the amount of issues that are being reported.
Content_zh: 所以考慮到目前回報的問題量，時間真的不多了。

Content: It's somewhat scary for sure.
Content_zh: 肯定有點可怕。

Content: So yeah, it's like a lot of people do tend to work, you know, late hours now, starting now but then, you know, also people
Content_zh: 所以現在開始很多人會加班到很晚，但同時也有人

Content: leave for Christmas break and stuff like that.
Content_zh: 也有人要去放聖誕假之類的。

Content: And so it's kind of a weird kind of thing going on.
Content_zh: 所以現在的狀況有點微妙。

Content: Franzke: Say something or are we just standing here?
Content_zh: Franzke：要說點什麼，還是我們就站在這裡？

Content: Schafer: Yeah, we're all going to stand.
Content_zh: Schafer：對，我們都要站著。

Content: Kipnis: What are we saying?
Content_zh: Kipnis：我們在說什麼？

Content: Schafer: Happy holidays, I think we say.
Content_zh: Schafer：我想我們要說「假期快樂」。

Content: That's what the Christmas card says.
Content_zh: 聖誕卡上就是這麼寫的。

Content: Well, I am going to say something.
Content_zh: 我要說點什麼。

Content: Rice: And then we'll all say "happy holidays".
Content_zh: Rice：然後我們大家一起說「假期快樂」。

Content: Schafer: This is all going in and out.
Content_zh: Schafer：這段都會剪進剪出吧。

Content: Hello, adventure gamers from everyone on the Broken Age team.
Content_zh: 各位冒險遊戲玩家好，這是《Broken Age》團隊全體的問候。

Content: We just wanted to say happy holidays.
Content_zh: 我們只是想祝大家假期快樂。

Content: Happy holidays? Happy holidays.
Content_zh: 假期快樂？假期快樂。

Content: Hey. Get back to work.
Content_zh: 嘿，回去工作吧。

Content: Finish it, finish it.
Content_zh: 把它做完，把它做完。

Content: Ryken: We had one little portion of the tree showing, so we had two little ornaments that we put on it.
Content_zh: Ryken：樹只露出一小塊，所以我們放了兩個小裝飾在上面。

Content: Their mind will fill in the rest, right?
Content_zh: 他們的腦袋會填滿剩下的，對吧？

Content: It'll be like, hey, great job.
Content_zh: 這會像，嘿，幹得好。

Content: [Music] Schafer: OK, hello, hello, everybody!
Content_zh: [音樂] Schafer：好了，哈囉，大家好！

Content: It's so nice to see you all.
Content_zh: 很高興見到大家。

Content: Everyone here?
Content_zh: 大家都在嗎？

Content: Everybody ready to go?
Content_zh: 大家都準備好了嗎？

Content: Hello, Happy New Year to you all.
Content_zh: 大家好，新年快樂。

Content: This is the first meeting since the new year began, right?
Content_zh: 這是新年開始後的第一次會議，對吧？

Content: Yeah, yeah, welcome back.
Content_zh: 對，對，歡迎回來。

Content: I'm so glad some of you survived the holidays and driving and you all decided to come work here for another year.
Content_zh: 很高興你們有些人熬過了假期和長途開車，還決定今年繼續回來這裡工作。

Content: Speaking of that, Bagel's actually been here 13 years.
Content_zh: 說到這個，Bagel 其實已經在這裡 13 年了。

Content: [Applause] Schafer: No, no.
Content_zh: [掌聲] Schafer：不，不。

Content: No clapping, he's not here.
Content_zh: 不拍手，他不在這裡。

Content: Sorry, Bagel, you're not here.
Content_zh: 抱歉，Bagel，你不在。

Content: Yeah, you can clap.
Content_zh: 對，你們可以拍手。

Content: He's actually coming up to visit.
Content_zh: 他其實要過來拜訪。

Content: Does anyone know when he's coming out to visit?
Content_zh: 有人知道他什麼時候要過來嗎？

Content: He's coming here on, Offscreen Voice: On his fourteenth year.
Content_zh: 他會在……畫外音：在他第十四年的時候。

Content: Schafer: And he'll be here for two weeks?
Content_zh: Schafer：他會在這待兩周嗎？

Content: Offscreen Voice: Until the 31st.
Content_zh: 畫外音：待到 31 號。

Content: Schafer: OK, awesome.
Content_zh: Schafer：好，太棒了。

Content: So then you can clap for him then.
Content_zh: 那到時候你們就可以替他拍手。

Content: Let's talk about Red's for a second, just 'cause it's launching next Tuesday.
Content_zh: 我們來聊一下 Red’s，因為它下週二就要上線了。

Content: To backers.
Content_zh: 給贊助者。

Content: We are shipping this thing to backers.
Content_zh: 我們要把這東西發給贊助者。

Content: And then on the 28th it's going to go out to the regular rest of the world.
Content_zh: 然後 28 號會正式發給其他所有人。

Content: But yeah, this is exciting.
Content_zh: 不過這很令人興奮。

Content: We have, we're all working on the Bugzilla right now.
Content_zh: 我們現在都在處理 Bugzilla。

Content: Everyone knows what they're doing pretty much, so why are having these days?
Content_zh: 大家基本上都知道自己在做什麼，所以我們為什麼還要開這幾天的會？

Content: I guess just to cheer everyone up.
Content_zh: 我猜只是想替大家打氣。

Content: Hey, you guys!
Content_zh: 你們好！

Content: We're going to ship a game.
Content_zh: 我們要推出遊戲了。

Content: Rice: Now I think let's talk about lockdown and stuff like that.
Content_zh: Rice：現在我想來談談封版之類的事。

Content: Schafer: And that. So lock it down, people.
Content_zh: Schafer：還有那個。各位，封版吧。

Content: Rice: Oliver, you want to talk about lockdown?
Content_zh: Rice：Oliver，你想談談封版嗎？

Content: Franzke: Sure. Lockdown, let's do it.
Content_zh: Franzke：好，封版，來吧。

Content: It's good. Right.
Content_zh: 很好。對。

Content: I mean everybody's still working and I, I mean I see like lots of things still happening this week.
Content_zh: 大家都還在工作，我也看到這週還有很多事在發生。

Content: However, I'm, I am going to get the release kind of branch ready.
Content_zh: 不過我會把發行用的分支準備好。

Content: Basically I'm going to manually integrate changes into the, like nothing goes into the branch without, you know, me looking at it,
Content_zh: 基本上我會手動把改動整合進去，任何東西進分支前都得先讓我看過。

Content: make sure that it's like, there are no risky changes going in.
Content_zh: 確保不會有高風險改動進去。

Content: And like the amount of risk is going to be reduced over time, for sure.
Content_zh: 而且風險量肯定會隨時間慢慢降低。

Content: Just to make sure that the build is as stable as possible.
Content_zh: 只是為了確保版本盡可能穩定。

Content: So just like how we do in the past for build days.
Content_zh: 就像我們以前在 build day 會做的那樣。

Content: Like we basically going to just try to play through the game and individual changes that need to go in for the 14th, they will be manually integrated.
Content_zh: 我們基本上會試著把遊戲跑完，而 14 號版本需要進去的個別改動，都會手動整合。

Content: Schafer: Talk to Oliver directly.
Content_zh: Schafer：直接跟 Oliver 說。

Content: And you'll make an angry face.
Content_zh: 你就會擺出生氣臉。

Content: Franzke: I will make angry face.
Content_zh: Franzke：我會擺出生氣臉。

Content: Schafer: Show me the face you have to see if you want to add, yeah.
Content_zh: Schafer：讓我看看，如果有人想加東西，得看到的那張臉。

Content: You'll have to look at that face if you want to add anything to the game after Thursday.
Content_zh: 如果你想在週四之後再加東西進遊戲，就得看著那張臉。

Content: Franzke: Right now Linux is driving me insane, but I'm sure, she'll do all good.
Content_zh: Franzke：現在 Linux 快把我逼瘋了，但我相信它最後會乖乖的。

Content: But other than that it looks pretty stable recently and I would like to keep it that way for sure.
Content_zh: 但除此之外，最近看起來很穩定，我很想保持這樣。

Content: Schafer: OK, great.
Content_zh: Schafer：好，太好了。

Content: Any other questions?
Content_zh: 還有其他問題嗎？

Content: All right.
Content_zh: 好。

Content: Go check Bugzilla.
Content_zh: 去看 Bugzilla。

Content: See you guys later.
Content_zh: 待會見。

Content: [Music] Crook: We're doing pretty good.
Content_zh: [音樂] Crook：我們進展不錯。

Content: We're at final stretch for the backer build.
Content_zh: 贊助者版本已經到最後衝刺了。

Content: So this will be a Beta build.
Content_zh: 所以這會是 Beta 版本。

Content: So it needs to be, not completely bug-free, but mostly.
Content_zh: 所以它不需要完全沒有 bug，但大致上要穩。

Content: Last night I got home about 1:00.
Content_zh: 昨晚我大概 1 點到家。

Content: I get home about 1:00 and then I couldn't sleep for about two, I just at in bed for two hours, just like thinking about today
Content_zh: 我大概 1 點到家，然後兩個小時睡不著，就躺在床上一直想今天，

Content: and what we had to get done and it's just like, just stupid.
Content_zh: 還有我們得完成的事，真的很蠢。

Content: I got to, you know; see my kids and my wife.
Content_zh: 我也得……你知道，去看看我的孩子和太太。

Content: So yeah, I have an aunt that's visiting right now, I haven't seen her yet.
Content_zh: 我有個阿姨現在來訪，我還沒見到她。

Content: She's been here for like four days and I haven't, I literally haven't seen her yet.
Content_zh: 她已經在這裡四天了，我真的還沒見到她。

Content: Crook: Yeah. Camden's getting hit probably the worst because cut scenes come in, you know, really late in the game.
Content_zh: Crook：對，Camden 可能被壓得最慘，因為過場動畫都到很後期才進來。

Content: Yeah, I think Camden has stayed here a couple nights.
Content_zh: 對，我想 Camden 已經有幾晚留在這裡了。

Content: Stoddard: I'm having to revisit a lot of things.
Content_zh: Stoddard：我得回頭重做很多東西。

Content: Things get redesigned or they get polished and timing has to move around, which is cool.
Content_zh: 東西會被重新設計或打磨，時間點也得跟著調整，這沒問題。

Content: But there's a lot.
Content_zh: 但真的很多。

Content: [Explosion sound] OK. That's fine.
Content_zh: [爆炸聲] 好，這樣可以。

Content: This is a hard, hard crunch but it's more about getting it right.
Content_zh: 這是很硬、很硬的趕工，但重點是把它做好。

Content: And I've been on other crunches where it wasn't about that, it was about we got it wrong and now we have to fix it.
Content_zh: 我也經歷過別的趕工，那不是為了做好，而是因為我們搞砸了，現在得修回來。

Content: I'm just here because I know if Double Fine makes it and really, really makes this happen, it's going to be amazing.
Content_zh: 我留在這裡，是因為我知道如果 Double Fine 撐過去，真的把這件事做成，那會非常了不起。

Content: It's going to be an amazing company.
Content_zh: Double Fine 會變成一間很棒的公司。

Content: I mean, it's already good, but we're just like struggling for years.
Content_zh: 我是說，它已經很好了，只是我們掙扎了好多年。

Content: And I think a lot of people here kind of hang on because like they know, they know Tim, they know, they know the management,
Content_zh: 我覺得這裡很多人會撐著，是因為他們了解 Tim，了解管理層，

Content: they know the vision and if it hits, it'll be really good.
Content_zh: 也了解這個願景。如果成功了，真的會很棒。

Content: It'll be really good.
Content_zh: 會很棒的。

Content: So we're just hanging in.
Content_zh: 所以我們就是撐著。

Content: Schafer: We are launching Red's tomorrow, crazy time.
Content_zh: Schafer：我們明天要推出 Red’s，真是瘋狂時刻。

Content: All of our backers.
Content_zh: 給我們所有贊助者。

Content: [Applause].
Content_zh: [掌聲]

Content: Thank you to our team for working so hard.
Content_zh: 謝謝團隊這麼努力。

Content: We are going to the backers tomorrow and the rest of the general population, future backers, if you will, on the 28th.
Content_zh: 明天會發給贊助者，28 號則發給一般大眾，也就是未來的贊助者，如果你要這麼說的話。

Content: Rice: Ah, we're about to launch a game.
Content_zh: Rice：啊，我們要發行一個遊戲了。

Content: In eight minutes.
Content_zh: 八分鐘後。

Content: I feel like, I feel like I'm kind of even in the same boat as backers, which is that's this, a really surreal thing that's happening.
Content_zh: 我覺得自己甚至跟贊助者有點在同一條船上，這件事真的很超現實。

Content: I don't know.
Content_zh: 我不知道。

Content: It's been a long time in the making, you know, it's two years almost now since we launched the Kickstarter and yeah.
Content_zh: 這一路做了很久，自從我們啟動 Kickstarter 以來，現在差不多快兩年了。

Content: Sometimes it just felt like it was never going to get here and now it's here and you know, this is something that really shouldn't exist.
Content_zh: 有時候真的覺得永遠等不到這一天，結果現在它到了。你知道，這東西其實本來不該存在。

Content: Like it's crazy that this exists and was able to be made this way and that the game that we've made is something that's coming out.
Content_zh: 這東西能存在、能用這種方式做出來，真的很瘋狂，而我們做出的遊戲現在真的要發行了。

Content: You know, I grew up playing Tim's old adventure games, young, like in elementary school.
Content_zh: 我從小就玩 Tim 以前的冒險遊戲，大概國小時就在玩。

Content: And I feel like they were kind of the games that showed me that games could be like an art or could be something different or something bigger.
Content_zh: 我覺得那些遊戲讓我看到，遊戲可以像藝術，也可以是不同的東西、更大的東西。

Content: And you know, the fact that I'm here now, like working on this game and putting this out
Content_zh: 你知道，現在我人在這裡，參與這款遊戲、把它推出去，

Content: is really a dream and the weirdest thing that's ever happened.
Content_zh: 真的像作夢，也是我遇過最奇怪的事。

Content: And the best thing that's ever happened to me.
Content_zh: 也是發生在我身上最美好的事。

Content: So it's exciting.
Content_zh: 所以很令人興奮。

Content: Schafer: All right. I've got to call my mom and say happy birthday.
Content_zh: Schafer：好，我得打給我媽說生日快樂。

Content: Rice: What?
Content_zh: Rice：什麼？

Content: Schafer: I got to call my mom and say happy birthday.
Content_zh: Schafer：我得打給我媽說生日快樂。

Content: Rice: Oh, yeah. Do that.
Content_zh: Rice：喔，對。快去。

Content: Schafer: We're releasing our game today.
Content_zh: Schafer：我們今天要發行遊戲。

Content: Yeah, well, it's, it should go out at 10:00, which is now so it's going to be out there.
Content_zh: 對，它應該 10 點上線，也就是現在，所以馬上就會公開了。

Content: And Dad can help you download it.
Content_zh: 爸爸可以幫你下載。

Content: OK, happy birthday, mom.
Content_zh: 好，生日快樂，媽。

Content: Rice: The game is up.
Content_zh: Rice：遊戲上線了。

Content: Preorders are up.
Content_zh: 預購也上線了。

Content: Schafer: Whoohoo! Rice: Yeah.
Content_zh: Schafer：嗚呼！Rice：耶。

Content: Schafer: All right, next game.
Content_zh: Schafer：好，下一款遊戲。

Content: Rice: Yeah, we're going to take everybody out.
Content_zh: Rice：對，我們要帶大家出去。

Content: So there's going to be a team lunch today, probably drinks later, too.
Content_zh: 所以今天會有團隊午餐，可能晚點也去喝一杯。

Content: I'm going out for drinks, so I hope people will join me.
Content_zh: 我會去喝一杯，希望大家一起來。

Content: But yeah, we're going to take everybody out for lunch and thank them for all the hard work
Content_zh: 但對，我們要帶大家出去吃午餐，謝謝他們的辛苦付出。

Content: and just take a nice breather before diving back into Two Act.
Content_zh: 在一頭栽回第二幕之前，好好喘口氣。

Content: Schafer: So thanks, everybody.
Content_zh: Schafer：謝謝各位。

Content: [Clinking glasses] [Music]
Content_zh: [碰杯聲] [音樂]

Content: Schafer: Hey it's Nathan Bagel Stapely!
Content_zh: Schafer：嘿，是 Nathan Bagel Stapley！

Content: [Applause] [Music]
Content_zh: [掌聲] [音樂]

Content: Schafer: This is downloading, start playing now.
Content_zh: Schafer：正在下載，現在開始玩。

Content: Let's hope you did a good job, Tim.
Content_zh: 希望你做得不錯，Tim。

Content: Me and my two girls sitting on my lap, can't wait.
Content_zh: 我和兩個女兒，她們坐在我腿上，等不及了。

Content: Sounds like I'd buy that for a dollar.
Content_zh: 聽起來像是「這我願意花一美元買」。

Content: Played 30 minutes, very happy so far.
Content_zh: 玩了 30 分鐘，目前很開心。

Content: Thanks to you and the Double Fine team. Rice: Yay!
Content_zh: 謝謝你和 Double Fine 團隊。Rice：耶！

Content: Schafer: It's like send any little bit of feedback.
Content_zh: Schafer：任何一點回饋都寄來吧。

Content: OK, I need some more proof that people are playing it.
Content_zh: 好，我需要更多證據，證明真的有人在玩。

Content: Rice: Tim, there's Twitch streams.
Content_zh: Rice：Tim，有 Twitch 實況。

Content: Schafer: Well, I don't want to watch that.
Content_zh: Schafer：嗯，我不想看那個。

Content: Well, maybe I do.
Content_zh: 嗯，也許我想看。

Content: Oh, man. This is weird.
Content_zh: 天啊，這感覺好怪。

Content: He's going to pick avalanche, no, foreign body.
Content_zh: 他要選 avalanche，不，是 foreign body。

Content: Foreign body, hull. Train.
Content_zh: Foreign body、hull、train。

Content: Foreign body, oh! Avalanche!
Content_zh: Foreign body，喔！Avalanche！

Content: That's good. Very good, avalanche.
Content_zh: 很好，非常好，avalanche。

Content: Now I'm totally into watching that.
Content_zh: 現在我完全看進去了。

Content: Schafer: What if he just said something really crappy right now.
Content_zh: Schafer：要是他現在說了一些壞話呢？

Content: Computer Voice: All right.
Content_zh: 電腦聲音：好。

Content: Looks like that'll be it, guys.
Content_zh: 看來就到這裡了，各位。

Content: Thanks a lot for watching and if anyone who is watching is involved in making this in any shape or form, then congratulations.
Content_zh: 非常感謝大家收看，如果正在看的任何人曾以任何形式參與製作，那恭喜你們。

Content: I think this is a winner.
Content_zh: 我覺得這款會成功。

Content: [Music] Game Character: I'm going to sit here forever.
Content_zh: [音樂] 遊戲角色：我要永遠坐在這裡。

Content: Offscreen Voice: But I don't know I need those yet.
Content_zh: 畫外音：可是我還不知道我需要那些東西。

Content: Schafer: Because you fell through the clouds.
Content_zh: Schafer：因為你從雲上掉下來了。

Content: Offscreen Voice: I don't know why my character knows that she needs them, 'cause she hasn't heard any...
Content_zh: 畫外音：我不知道為什麼我的角色會知道她需要它們，因為她還沒聽到任何……

Content: Schafer: She's just interested in shoes.
Content_zh: Schafer：她只是對鞋子有興趣。

Content: Offscreen Voice: No, that's not true.
Content_zh: 畫外音：不，不是這樣。

Content: Schafer: And she fell through the clouds.
Content_zh: Schafer：而且她從雲上掉下來了。

Content: Offscreen Voice: The other guys in there said that I needed shoes.
Content_zh: 畫外音：裡面的其他人說我需要鞋子。

Content: Schafer: Yeah.
Content_zh: Schafer：是啊。

Content: Offscreen Voice: I don't know if I clicked on that dialogue option, though.
Content_zh: 畫外音：但我不確定我有沒有點過那個對話選項。

Content: Game Character: What are you doing down here?
Content_zh: 遊戲角色：妳在下面做什麼？

Content: Vella: I'm an offering for the Maidens Feast.
Content_zh: Vella：我是 Maidens Feast 的祭品。

Content: Schafer: She fell through the clouds.
Content_zh: Schafer：她從雲上掉下來了。

Content: Obviously she knows she needs cloud shoes.
Content_zh: 顯然她知道自己需要雲鞋。

Content: You're playing it wrong.
Content_zh: 你玩錯了。

Content: Game Character: Kind of shabby looking for a Maidens Feast.
Content_zh: 遊戲角色：以 Maidens Feast 來說，看起來有點寒酸。
